task_tokens_consumed: Is an attention-distribution-fitting + tail-parameter-renormalization mechanism for LLM attention anticipated by 2024-2026 prior art?

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Heavy-tailed attention analysis and power-law fitting
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Empirical and theoretical studies fitting attention score distributions to heavy-tailed / power-law / Zipfian families and exploiting the tail for compression or selection.
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: attention-distribution-fitting (parametric characterization of attention mass via heavy-tailed laws)

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Sparse / top-k / threshold attention with tail-budget bounds
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Efficiency methods that truncate the attention tail under a budget while bounding approximation error, including top-k, threshold, and adaptive-sparsity attention.
expected_paper_signatures: Reformer / Routing Transformer style top-k attention (Kitaev et al.)
Adaptively Sparse Transformers (Correia et al.); Quest: Query-Aware Sparsity (2024)
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: tail-budget bound for attention efficiency (S_efficiency scenario)

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: KV-cache eviction / tail-token retention for long context
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Long-context inference methods that score tokens by attention-tail importance and retain or evict KV entries, directly relevant to needle-in-haystack capability.
expected_paper_signatures: H2O: Heavy-Hitter Oracle for KV Cache (Zhang et al., 2023)
StreamingLLM with Attention Sinks (Xiao et al., 2024)
SnapKV / Scissorhands / KIVI tail-aware KV compression (2024)

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Softmax / temperature / entropy renormalization of attention
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Mechanisms that rescale, sharpen, or renormalize the attention softmax via learned temperatures, entropy regularizers, or post-hoc parameter adjustment to reshape the tail.
expected_paper_signatures: Differentiable Top-k via Perturbed Maximum (Cordonnier et al.)
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: tail-parameter-renormalization (post-fit reweighting of attention distribution parameters)

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Calibration / outlier renormalization of attention logits and weights
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Training-free or quantization-aware methods that detect outlier attention logits/heads and renormalize their parameters to stabilize the tail (often for quantization or long-context drift).
expected_paper_signatures: Massive Activations in Large Language Models (Sun et al., 2024)
SmoothQuant / AWQ outlier renormalization (Xiao et al.; Lin et al.)
Attention Sink Calibration / LM-Infinite (Han et al., 2023)
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: tail-parameter-renormalization via outlier/sink correction in attention parameters

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Mixture / parametric fits of attention as density estimation
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Works that explicitly model attention as a mixture or parametric density (Gaussian/Laplace/Pareto mixtures, kernelized attention) and re-fit components, including tail components, for efficiency or expressivity.
expected_paper_signatures: Performer / Linear Attention via Kernel Density (Choromanski et al.)
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: attention-distribution-fitting via explicit parametric/mixture density models

landscape_completeness_claim: These six clusters jointly span the two named sub-mechanisms (distribution-fitting and tail-renormalization) across both efficiency and long-context capability scenarios, making it highly likely that at least one cluster yields a paper at functional_similarity >= 0.7 and that the K-1 joint-coverage criterion is met by C2, C3, or C5.
